column1: One row per local authority. Year-by-year planning applications, MCS heat-pump installations, and BUS redemptions. 2009-2025 for planning and MCS; BUS started in 2022/23.

column2: Planning apps over the planning-scrape window.
MCS installs over the same window.
Planning apps as % of MCS installs (2015-2025).

Our scrape of the PlanIt API (planit.org.uk/api), via scripts/scrape_applications_ashp.py and scripts/scrape_applications_heat_pump_broad.py. Two passes — narrow ASHP/"air source heat pump" terms then broader "heat pump"/GSHP/WSHP terms. Multi-day rate-limited run. 30,446 apps in the merged DB.

Manual export from the MCS Data Dashboard (datadashboard.mcscertified.com/InstallationInsights), saved to data/external_heat_pump_data/MCS_heatpump_installations_by_LA_by_year.csv. Every heat pump fitted by an MCS-accredited installer.

DESNZ Boiler Upgrade Scheme statistics, monthly publication on gov.uk. Source xlsx (BUS_March_2026.xlsx) is the March 2026 release; the CSVs in data/external_heat_pump_data/ are sheets extracted from it. England + Wales only — the scheme doesn't cover Scotland or NI.
MCS's current LA list (post-April-2023 unitaries, e.g. Somerset, North Yorkshire, Cumberland). The row label.
What PlanIt calls each council. PlanIt mirrors portal names, which often still show pre-reorg districts.
Most councils, e.g. la_name "Cornwall" pairs with "cornwall".
MCS uses the unitary; PlanIt has the old districts. We sum the districts' apps into the unitary row.

column1: Joint PlanIt authority → multiple MCS LAs
column2: We split PlanIt apps across MCS LAs by install share. 6 cases: Adur and Worthing, Babergh Mid Suffolk, Bromsgrove Redditch, Mid Kent, South Norfolk Broadland, South West Devon. Flagged via has_joint_planit_authority.
Both datasets have non-zero numbers.
MCS installs, no planning apps (7 councils).
Planning apps, no MCS installs in-window (18 councils).
Planning apps, council not in MCS's list at all (NI, some Scottish, Isles of Scilly — 21 councils).

column1: Planning scrape only goes back to 2015, so 2009-2014 always show 0 apps.
Inner-London ratios look extreme because MCS counts are tiny there and most heat pumps get folded into bigger applications.
Joint-authority split can shift a single app via rounding (29,715 in sheet vs 29,716 actual).
National parks have their own rows (~1,170 apps) but no MCS data; surrounding councils are slightly under-counted as a result.
18 English county councils show small app counts (~113 total) with no MCS match — non-residential applications on county-owned land.
Nuneaton and Bedworth: 235 MCS installs, 0 planning apps — likely a scrape coverage gap.
Suspiciously low planning shares (Boston 0.8%, S Holland 1.0%, Tamworth 1.2%, Middlesbrough 1.2%, Fenland 1.4%) are most likely partial scrape coverage. Median across matched councils is ~7%.
BUS columns are blank for Scotland and NI (out of scheme).
Blank cells inside BUS columns elsewhere are ONS suppression ([c1]/[c2] in the source) — below disclosure threshold, not zero.

column2: /tmp/ashp.db (PlanIt scrape, 30,446 apps)
